title: padus racemosa f. aurea
intro: Padus racemosa f. aurea, also known as the golden forsythia or golden alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with bright yellow flowers, belonging to the family Oleaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Padus racemosa f. aurea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
